rice.name: Rice
rice.soil_type: Clay or loamy soil with good water retention
rice.water_requirement: High - 1200 to 2000 mm of rainfall needed
steps: Prepare the field by plowing and leveling it properly.
Soak seeds for 24 hours before sowing.
Transplant seedlings to main field after 25-30 days.
Maintain 5 cm of standing water during tillering stage.
Apply fertilizers in split doses: at transplanting, tillering, and panicle initiation.
Harvest when 80% grains turn golden yellow (around 110-145 days).
rice.tips: Rice grows best in warm, humid conditions. Ensure proper water management to avoid water stress.

wheat.name: Wheat
wheat.soil_type: Loamy or clay loam soil with good drainage
wheat.water_requirement: Medium - 450 to 650 mm of water needed
wheat.fertilizer: Urea for Nitrogen, DAP for Phosphorus and Potassium
steps: Plow and prepare the field in late October.
Sow seeds at 100-125 kg per hectare, 5 cm deep.
First irrigation (crown root initiation) after 20-25 days.
Apply nitrogen in 2-3 splits for best results.
Control weeds in the first 30-35 days.
Harvest when grains are hard and moisture is below 14% (around 120-150 days).
wheat.tips: Wheat requires cool weather during growing season and dry weather at harvest time.

maize.name: Maize (Corn)
maize.water_requirement: Medium - 500 to 800 mm of water needed
steps: Deep plow the field and add organic compost.
Sow seeds 4-5 cm deep at 60x20 cm spacing.
First irrigation immediately after sowing.
Apply nitrogen fertilizer at knee-high stage.
Earth up plants at 30 days to support stalk.
Harvest when husks turn brown and grains are hard (90-110 days).
maize.tips: Maize is a heavy feeder. Ensure good drainage as it cannot tolerate waterlogging.

cotton.name: Cotton
cotton.soil_type: Deep black cotton soil or loamy soil, pH 6.0-8.0
steps: Plow field deeply (30-40 cm) and prepare fine tilth.
Sow BT cotton seeds at 90x60 cm spacing.
Thin to one plant per hill after 15 days.
Apply basal dose of fertilizer at sowing.
Irrigate every 10-15 days depending on soil moisture.
Pick cotton when bolls are fully open (160-180 days).
cotton.tips: Cotton requires long, warm growing season. Avoid waterlogging especially during early growth.

sugarcane.name: Sugarcane
sugarcane.fertilizer: High Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Potassium; also needs Zinc and Sulfur
steps: Select disease-free 2-3 budded setts for planting.
Plant in furrows 90 cm apart, 5-8 cm deep.
Cover with soil and irrigate immediately.
Apply nitrogen in 3 splits: at planting, 60 and 120 days.
Earth up canes at 90 days to prevent lodging.
Harvest after 10-12 months when sugar content is maximum.
sugarcane.tips: Sugarcane is a long-duration crop. Ratoon crop can be taken after first harvest to save costs.

soybean.name: Soybean
soybean.soil_type: Well-drained loamy or clay loam soil, pH 6.0-7.5
soybean.fertilizer: Low Nitrogen (fixes own N), Phosphorus (DAP), Potassium (MOP)
steps: Inoculate seeds with Rhizobium culture before sowing.
Sow seeds at 45x5 cm spacing, 3-4 cm deep.
Apply basal dose of Phosphorus and Potassium.
Control weeds in first 30-45 days (critical period).
Irrigate at flowering and pod-filling stages.
Harvest when 95% of pods turn brown (90-100 days).
soybean.tips: Soybean is a nitrogen-fixing legume. It improves soil fertility for subsequent crops.

potato.name: Potato
potato.soil_type: Sandy loam or loam soil, pH 5.2-6.4
potato.water_requirement: Medium - 500 to 700 mm, requires frequent irrigation
potato.fertilizer: High Nitrogen and Potassium, moderate Phosphorus
steps: Prepare well-tilled, loose, friable soil.
Cut seed tubers ensuring each piece has 2-3 eyes.
Plant at 60x20 cm spacing, 10-15 cm deep.
First earthing up at 30 days, second at 45-50 days.
Apply nitrogen in 2 splits: at planting and after first earthing.
Harvest when tops dry out and skins are set (70-90 days).
potato.tips: Potatoes need cool weather and well-aerated soil. Avoid planting in poorly drained fields.

tomato.name: Tomato
tomato.season: Can be grown year-round with irrigation; best Nov-Jan
tomato.soil_type: Well-drained sandy loam or loam soil, pH 6.0-7.0
tomato.water_requirement: Medium - 400 to 600 mm; requires regular irrigation
steps: Raise seedlings in nursery beds or pro-trays for 25-30 days.
Transplant to main field at 75x60 cm spacing.
Install bamboo stakes for support after transplanting.
Apply fertilizer in 4-5 splits throughout growth.
Irrigate every 7-10 days, avoid over-watering.
Harvest when fruits are firm and turning red (60-80 days after transplanting).
tomato.tips: Tomatoes need full sun and consistent moisture. Irregular watering causes blossom end rot.

groundnut.name: Groundnut (Peanut)
groundnut.fertilizer: Low Nitrogen, Phosphorus (SSP), Potassium, Gypsum at pegging stage
steps: Shell pods just before sowing; do not damage seed coat.
Sow seeds at 30x10 cm spacing, 4-5 cm deep.
Apply gypsum (500 kg/ha) at the time of pegging (35-40 days).
Control weeds in first 45 days; avoid disturbing during pegging.
Irrigate at critical stages: flowering, pegging, and pod development.
Harvest when inner shell shows dark veining (110-130 days).
groundnut.tips: Groundnut fixes nitrogen. Light sandy soils allow easy pod penetration and harvesting.

mango.name: Mango
mango.water_requirement: Low-Medium - 1000 to 1500 mm; dry period needed for flowering
mango.fertilizer: NPK in 3 split doses per year; Zinc and Boron for fruit quality
steps: Plant grafted saplings in 1x1x1 m pits during July-August.
Space trees at 10x10 m (or 5x5 m for high density).
Apply organic manure in first pit at planting time.
Irrigate regularly for first 2-3 years; reduce water before flowering.
Prune dead and criss-crossing branches after harvest.
Harvest when fruits turn yellowish-green with specific gravity > 1.01 (4-5 months after flowering).
mango.tips: Mango trees need a dry period before flowering. Avoid waterlogging at any stage.

urea.name: Urea
urea.use: Applied for vegetative growth, greening of leaves
urea.precaution: Apply when soil is moist; avoid over-application as it burns roots

dap.name: DAP (Di-Ammonium Phosphate)
dap.use: Applied at sowing/planting for root development and early growth
dap.precaution: Apply as basal dose; mix well with soil before planting

mop.name: MOP (Muriate of Potash)
mop.use: Improves fruit quality, disease resistance, and water use efficiency
mop.precaution: Avoid application in saline soils; apply with irrigation water

ssp.name: SSP (Single Super Phosphate)
ssp.use: Good source of both phosphorus and sulfur; used for oilseeds
ssp.precaution: Good for sulfur-deficient soils; apply as basal dose

organic.name: Organic / FYM (Farmyard Manure)
organic.nutrient: Low NPK but improves soil structure and microbial activity
organic.use: Applied before planting to improve soil health; provides slow-release nutrition
organic.precaution: Use well-decomposed FYM; fresh manure can burn crops
